subject: [PATCH v6 1/2] mm: track DONTCACHE dirty pages per bdi_writeback

2026-05-05.patch_summary: This patch adds a new counter to track the number of dirty pages with the dropbehind flag set, which are written via RWF_DONTCACHE. The counter is used by the writeback flusher to determine how many pages to write back when expediting writeback for IOCB_DONTCACHE writes. This allows for more efficient handling of these types of writes without flushing the entire BDI's dirty pages. The patch introduces a new enum value WB_DONTCACHE_DIRTY and updates several functions in mm/filemap.c and mm/page-writeback.c to increment or decrement this counter as necessary.

reviews.author: Jan Kara
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that folio_test_clear_dropbehind() can be called concurrently by multiple lookups, and suggested adding a lock to prevent double-decrementing of WB_DONTCACHE_DIRTY counter.
reviews.raw_body:
Yeah, good catch with the folio_test_clear_dropbehind(). Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>

								Honza

2026-05-06.patch_summary: This patch adds a new counter to track the number of dirty pages with the dropbehind flag set, which is used by RWF_DONTCACHE writes. The counter, WB_DONTCACHE_DIRTY, is incremented when a page is dirtied via RWF_DONTCACHE and decremented when it's cleaned or cleared. This allows the writeback flusher to expedite writeback for IOCB_DONTCACHE writes without flushing the entire BDI's dirty pages. The patch modifies several kernel functions to update this counter correctly, ensuring accurate tracking of these special dirty pages.
